summary: The papers in this cluster share a focus on the ecological impacts of anthropogenic stressors on freshwater algal communities, specifically examining species-level responses to environmental perturbation. Research centered on the epilithic diatom *Achnanthes minutissima*, identified as a dominant, acid-tolerant colonizer, and the green alga *Coenochloris polycocca*, which exhibited differential sensitivity to pH reduction. Experimental acidification was a key methodological approach, conducted within mesocosm systems using artificial substrates to track community succession over time. The geographic scope was restricted to the Togyo reservoir near the Korean Demilitarized Zone, a region under unique ecological regulation due to restricted human access.
